metadata.credit: Data and analysis provided by Draftsim
metadata.author: Bryan Hohns (Veveil)

set_overview.summary: ONE is a fast, aggressive format with limited blocking viability due to combat tricks attached to card draw. The set offers strategic depth through mechanic intersections (toxic, corrupted, oil, proliferate) and ample mana fixing. Balance early pressure with high-end power.

color_notes.{R} Red: Versatile and aggressive. Excellent common removal (Hexgold Slash, Volt Charge), solid creatures, oil support, and the anti-artifact Shrapnel Slinger. Lacks toxic synergy.
color_notes.{W} White: Aggressive with strong toxic focus. Provides Phyrexian Mite tokens. Premium removal in Planar Disruption and tricks like Compleat Devotion.
color_notes.{G} Green: Midrange with oil and toxic themes. Chunky toxic creatures (Plague Nurse, Tyrranax Atrocity). Limited interaction (Ruthless Predation, Carnivorous Canopy).
color_notes.{B} Black: Toxic and removal-focused. Exceptional common Anoint with Affliction but lacks premium alternatives.
color_notes.{U} Blue: Weakest color. Proliferate and artifact focus; poor early game. Best as support color in Sealed or dedicated artifact decks.

key_gameplay_notes: Respect the aggressive nature — early plays matter significantly.
Combat tricks with card draw create unavoidable blocking risk; play defensively.
Proliferate is better as incidental than built-around.
Three distinct toxic paces exist (aggressive White, midrange Green, defensive Blue/Black).
Mana fixing is plentiful — splashing is viable.

mechanics.name: Toxic
mechanics.summary: Creatures deal poison damage alongside regular damage. Ten poison counters lose the game.
mechanics.performance: Format-defining win condition. Three flavors (aggressive small / midrange / tempo).

mechanics.name: Corrupted
mechanics.summary: Triggers when the opponent has 3+ poison counters.
mechanics.performance: Modest effects; requires early board presence.

mechanics.name: Oil Counters
mechanics.summary: Placed on creatures/permanents for synergistic payoffs. Encourages creature-based strategies with proliferate.
mechanics.performance: Core to Gruul (best deck in format).

mechanics.name: Proliferate
mechanics.summary: Add a counter to each permanent and player with counters of any kind that you choose.
mechanics.performance: Supplements other strategies rather than dominating.

mechanics.name: For Mirrodin!
mechanics.summary: Equipment that creates a creature token and equips itself. Primarily Boros/Azorius.

archetypes.name: Azorius Artifacts
archetypes.strategy: Aggressive tempo deck leveraging artifact payoffs and evasion. Target 13+ artifacts.

archetypes.name: Dimir Proliferate / Control
archetypes.strategy: Control-focused. Fliers, removal, incidental poison accumulation.

archetypes.name: Rakdos Sacrifice ("Eggs")
archetypes.strategy: Grindy attrition with sacrifice synergies and card-draw engines.

archetypes.name: Gruul Oil
archetypes.strategy: Best deck in the format. Proactive creature strategy with proliferate overlay.

archetypes.name: Selesnya Toxic (Go Wide)
archetypes.strategy: Wide token strategy channeling poison through Phyrexian Mites.

archetypes.name: Orzhov Toxic (Corrupted)
archetypes.strategy: Slower poison accumulation leveraging corrupted triggers.

archetypes.name: Izzet Spells
archetypes.strategy: Tempo-focused spell deck with creature support.

archetypes.name: Golgari Toxic (Go Tall)
archetypes.strategy: Single large poison creature strategy with support and protection.

archetypes.name: Boros Equipment
archetypes.strategy: Equipment-focused aggressive strategy using the For Mirrodin! theme.

archetypes.name: Simic Proliferate / Tempo
archetypes.strategy: Tempo strategy with proliferate overlay and creature focus.

top_uncommons.name: Annex Sentry
top_uncommons.note: #1 — busted uncommon with 4 toughness and toxic 1.

top_uncommons.name: Armored Scrapgorger
top_uncommons.note: #8 — efficient mana dork that upgrades itself.

general_tips: Respect the aggressive nature — early plays matter.
Mana fixing is plentiful; splash when correct.
Combat tricks with card draw make blocking risky.
Proliferate is better as incidental than built-around.
Prioritize removal heavily — interaction defines matchups.
Factor poison accumulation into blocking decisions; 10 poison = loss regardless of life total.
Avoid greedy hands; mana consistency is crucial in this fast format.
